message:
fix(service): check filter predicate before readiness; respect strategy exclusion in load balancer fallback

Filter: call() now evaluates the predicate before checking ready_observed.
Previously, calling a Filter without poll_ready returned NotReady even for
requests that would be rejected by the predicate. This wasted a readiness
cycle on requests that should have been rejected immediately. The new
order: reject first (no readiness consumed), then check readiness, then
dispatch. Test added verifying rejected requests never poll inner readiness.

LoadBalancer: Strategy trait gains a permits_index method (default: bounds
check) that call_balanced uses during its readiness fallback probe loop.
The Weighted strategy overrides this to return false for zero-weight
backends, preventing the fallback from selecting a backend that the
strategy would never pick. Test added verifying a zero-weight ready
backend is skipped during weighted fallback, reaching the next selectable
backend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
